data.title: S&P 500 workforce shrinks in 2025 for first time since 2016
data.description:
Employment across S&P 500 (SP500) companies declined in 2025, marking the first annual drop in nearly a decade, according to Bank of America Global Research, with data compiled alongside Bloomberg.

Total headcount slipped to about 28.1M, ending a multi-year expansion that had steadily lifted corporate payrolls since the pandemic recovery.

The reversal suggests a shift in corporate strategy as firms prioritize efficiency, automation, and margin protection amid a slowing macro backdrop. Layoffs have been concentrated in sectors such as technology and financials, where cost discipline and restructuring efforts have intensified.

While the decline is modest relative to the broader labor market, it underscores a cooling in hiring appetite among large-cap companies that have historically driven employment growth.

Here is a chart posted by Mike Zaccardi on X:

data.title: Wall Street mixed as Israel-Lebanon truce gets extended
data.description:
The stock market opened mixed on Friday as the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon got extended by three weeks. 

The S&P 500 (SP500) +0.2%, the Dow (DJI) -0.3%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.6%. 

President Donald Trump said in a social media post that he had ordered the U.S. Navy to “shoot and kill any boat” caught laying mines in the strait.

Treasury yields edged slightly lower across the curve. The 2-year yield (US2Y) dipped to 3.84%, while the 10-year yield (US10Y) eased to 4.33%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) also inched down to 4.91%.

The economic calendar for the day includes the consumer sentiment report.

data.title: Pony AI gains after updating on its robotaxi progress
data.description:
Pony AI (PONY) used the Auto China 2026 conference in Beijing to highlight the company's stronger push toward large-scale commercialization of its autonomous driving business, led by lower-cost Gen-7 robotaxis, fleet expansion, and a more efficient AI training system. Notably, the company highlighted that the total cost of its 2027 robotaxi in China, including the base vehicle and autonomous driving kit, is expected to fall below RMB230K while its fleet has grown to 1,400 vehicles from 270 and user registrations have surpassed 1M.

In terms of profitability, the company noted that its Gen-7 kit has delivered a 70% reduction in bill-of-materials costs, helping it reach unit-economics breakeven in two major Southern China markets. Pony AI (PONY) said it plans to expand the fleet to more than 3K robotaxis by the end of this year and grow to 20 cities globally, with nearly half overseas, while also preparing an international version of its Gen-7 platform for local market requirements.

"Today, the question is no longer whether Robotaxi can work. The focus is on how to scale it safely, efficiently, and at the right cost," updated Pony AI (PONY) CEO Dr. James Peng.

Shares of Pony AI (PONY) were up 2% in premarket action.
